jokes.text: I wouldn't shop at that big mega-store if you paid me.
jokes.setup: Discussion about competing with a new sporting goods mega-store
jokes.punchline: The ironic contradiction - she wouldn't shop there even if literally paid to do so
jokes.explanation: Simple exaggeration for emphasis, mildly amusing but predictable

jokes.text: You know, everyone's a Democrat till they get a little money. Then they come to their senses.
jokes.setup: Lindsay declares she's a Democrat, Harold responds with political wisdom
jokes.punchline: The implication that being Republican is 'coming to your senses' when you have money
jokes.explanation: Classic dad political humor, well-delivered and character-appropriate

jokes.text: The porn star?
jokes.setup: Someone mentions George Bush is coming to speak
jokes.punchline: Daniel's confusion about which George Bush, defaulting to the adult film actor
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Daniel would absolutely think of the porn star first. Great economy and timing.

jokes.text: Don't worry about it, Amy. Bush probably doesn't know anything about the tuba.
jokes.setup: Amy is nervous about playing tuba for Bush's arrival
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's matter-of-fact reassurance that the Vice President probably lacks tuba expertise
jokes.explanation: Nice deadpan delivery with logical absurdity - why would a VP know about tubas?

jokes.text: Amy mimics 'Hail to the Chief' out of tune on tuba
jokes.setup: Discussion of Amy's nervousness about playing for Bush
jokes.punchline: Amy demonstrates by badly mimicking the presidential song
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that shows Amy's anxiety in a relatable way. Visual gag works well.

jokes.text: How we ever gonna plan our coup?
jokes.setup: Secret Service is clearing areas before Bush's visit
jokes.punchline: Daniel sarcastically asks how they'll plan their government overthrow
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with perfect Daniel deadpan. Subversive and character-specific.

jokes.text: COOOOOUP. COUP. COUP.
jokes.setup: Vice Principal warns Daniel not to joke about coups
jokes.punchline: Daniel immediately does exactly what he was told not to do, with emphasis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Daniel moment - rebellious, defiant, and perfectly timed escalation

jokes.text: Once you start down that dark path, forever will it dominate your destiny. At least that's what Yoda always says.
jokes.setup: Geeks observing the popular kids' table dynamics
jokes.punchline: Bill applying Yoda wisdom to high school social hierarchies
jokes.explanation: Great nerdy character moment mixing Star Wars with teenage social commentary

jokes.text: I mean, not all cheerleaders have to be pretty, but their cheerleaders aren't even remotely pretty. I mean, they're dirty.
jokes.setup: Todd criticizing opposing team's cheerleaders
jokes.punchline: The escalation from 'not pretty' to 'not even remotely pretty' to 'dirty'
jokes.explanation: Mean-spirited humor that reveals Todd's character but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: Hey, not all our cheerleaders are pretty. / You can say that again.
jokes.setup: Girl defends their school's cheerleaders
jokes.punchline: Todd immediately agrees, insulting his own school's team
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline structure showing Todd's social obliviousness

jokes.text: Sam, you're not gonna defend me?
jokes.setup: Todd insults cheerleaders, Cindy expects Sam to defend her
jokes.punchline: Sam's complete inability to handle the social situation
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Sam's awkwardness and inexperience with relationship dynamics

jokes.text: I like Sam. / What, do you want to see us fight? / I'd like to see that.
jokes.setup: Girl expresses liking Sam, Todd asks if she wants to see them fight
jokes.punchline: Girl immediately confirms she would enjoy seeing violence
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about teenage bloodlust, well-structured escalation

jokes.text: MOUSETRAP! I WIN!
jokes.setup: Geeks playing board games
jokes.punchline: Bill's over-the-top excitement about winning a children's game
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the geeks' simple pleasures and Bill's enthusiasm

jokes.text: Maybe you can get the school to start a team. / Funny.
jokes.setup: Neal sarcastically suggests a Mousetrap team
jokes.punchline: Bill's dry response to the obvious sarcasm
jokes.explanation: Nice interplay between characters, Bill's deadpan works well

jokes.text: I mean, I'm good at Mousetrap, and you're really good at Kerplunk.
jokes.setup: Bill trying to console Sam about losing at Mousetrap
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of having specialized skills in children's board games
jokes.explanation: Sweet and absurd, showing Bill's earnest attempt to be supportive

jokes.text: All she ever wants to do is make out and stuff. / I'd kill to be that bored.
jokes.setup: Sam complains his girlfriend only wants physical intimacy
jokes.punchline: Neal's immediate response that he'd love to have that 'problem'
jokes.explanation: Perfect teenage boy logic and great contrast between Sam's naivety and Neal's horniness

jokes.text: We went to the mall twice, and we went to this football game once, and after that, we went to a different mall.
jokes.setup: Sam describing his dates with Cindy
jokes.punchline: The progression ending with going to 'a different mall' as if that's variety
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that captures suburban teenage dating perfectly. The 'different mall' is the punchline.

jokes.text: That sounds fantastic.
jokes.setup: Sam's boring date description
jokes.punchline: Bill's completely deadpan sarcastic response
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery that punctuates Sam's obliviousness

jokes.text: Because she's a goddess! Am I the last sane man on this godforsaken planet?
jokes.setup: Discussion of why Sam dates Cindy despite incompatibility
jokes.punchline: Neal's over-the-top proclamation about being the only sane one
jokes.explanation: Great Neal outburst showing his frustration with his friends' romantic 'problems'

jokes.text: Hey, pass her over here. I'd move to the mall if she wanted me to.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Sam not appreciating Cindy
jokes.punchline: Bill's willingness to literally relocate to a mall for her
jokes.explanation: Clever callback to the mall joke with Bill's characteristic over-commitment

jokes.text: Um, 'cause I hate it there?
jokes.setup: Lindsay asks why they never hang out at Ken's house
jokes.punchline: Ken's matter-of-fact response with a question mark, as if it's obvious
jokes.explanation: Good Ken moment - blunt honesty delivered with perfect timing

jokes.text: How come everyone always thinks I'm joking all the time? Something to do with my voice, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Ken complains about not being taken seriously
jokes.punchline: His self-aware analysis of his own vocal delivery
jokes.explanation: Meta joke that works because it's true - Ken does have a naturally sarcastic delivery

jokes.text: I guess raising me wasn't one of the things they learned to do in college.
jokes.setup: Ken explaining his parents are good at their jobs
jokes.punchline: The implication that parenting isn't a skill they acquired through education
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny and sad commentary on neglectful parents, perfectly delivered

jokes.text: She was the best mommy money could buy.
jokes.setup: Ken talking about his nanny Catherine
jokes.punchline: The bitter irony of commercializing maternal care
jokes.explanation: Brilliant line that's both funny and heartbreaking. Perfect economy and character insight.

jokes.text: But when I was born... I was born with both male and female parts.
jokes.setup: Amy nervously builds up to sharing something personal with Ken
jokes.punchline: Not a punchline per se, but the uncomfortable revelation creates cringe comedy
jokes.explanation: Groundbreaking TV moment handled with sensitivity. The comedy comes from the awkwardness, not mockery.

jokes.text: If I was dating you when you were just born, things might be a little different 'cause of all that stuff. But now you're all girl now, so it's...OK.
jokes.setup: Ken trying to process Amy's revelation
jokes.punchline: His completely inappropriate response about dating a baby
jokes.explanation: Peak Ken awkwardness - trying to be supportive but saying exactly the wrong thing

jokes.text: I had my appendix out, so, you know, I've been there.
jokes.setup: Ken trying to relate to Amy's intersex experience
jokes.punchline: Comparing intersex surgery to an appendectomy
jokes.explanation: Absolutely perfect Ken moment - completely tone-deaf comparison delivered with total sincerity

jokes.text: Maybe because he's the second most powerful man on Earth.
jokes.setup: Lindsay doesn't want to ask Bush a question because she's a Democrat
jokes.punchline: Rosso's dramatic escalation about Bush's importance
jokes.explanation: Standard dramatic escalation that fits Rosso's passionate character

jokes.text: Maybe because we live in a country where we can actually question our leaders without fear of being hacked to death by a machete.
jokes.setup: Continuing his speech about why Lindsay should talk to Bush
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific and dramatic image of machete death
jokes.explanation: Classic Rosso over-the-top imagery that's both patriotic and absurd

jokes.text: I guess me and my hippie friends were all just wasting our time at Berkeley demonstrating and stopping an unjust war.
jokes.setup: Rosso getting frustrated with Lindsay's lack of patriotic enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: His sarcastic dismissal of his own anti-war activism
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rosso's contradictory nature between past and present

jokes.text: I've got the best job in the world! 12 grand a year, and I'm overpaid.
jokes.setup: Rosso excited about getting Lindsay to do the Q&A
jokes.punchline: The contrast between 'best job' and being overpaid at $12,000
jokes.explanation: Sharp commentary on teacher pay with good ironic structure

jokes.text: But, uh, where am I supposed to work? / Sorry, sir, it's not my problem.
jokes.setup: Secret Service kicks Rosso out of his own office
jokes.punchline: Agent's complete indifference to Rosso's work situation
jokes.explanation: Good bureaucratic indifference humor, though fairly standard

jokes.text: Uh, Crockpot, you're going to get a visual on that bogie. He's coming at you. He's about 6'3", a real Dr. Feelgood look. Check him out.
jokes.setup: Secret Service using code names and descriptions
jokes.punchline: Describing someone as having a 'Dr. Feelgood look' in official security language
jokes.explanation: Funny mixing of official security jargon with casual pop culture references

jokes.text: I thought the cafeteria food looked a little gross, so I had an apple. / It was actually pretty good. If you like Salisbury steak.
jokes.setup: Sam says he had an apple because cafeteria food looked gross
jokes.punchline: Cindy reveals she ate the Salisbury steak and liked it
jokes.explanation: Mild misdirection showing their different approaches to cafeteria food

jokes.text: They say that Reagan had Iran delay the release of the hostages until after he was elected. You should ask him about that. I bet he flips.
jokes.setup: Lindsay asking for tough questions for Bush
jokes.punchline: Daniel's suggestion to ask about a conspiracy theory to make the VP 'flip'
jokes.explanation: Good Daniel moment - he wants to cause political chaos just for entertainment

jokes.text: Oh, Lindsay, that's boring. Nobody wants to hear about that.
jokes.setup: Lindsay wants to ask about trickle-down economics
jokes.punchline: Kim dismissing actual policy discussion as boring
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kim - preferring conspiracy theories to actual economics

jokes.text: I want to ask him about the aliens at Roswell.
jokes.setup: Suggesting questions for the Vice President
jokes.punchline: Kim's priority is asking about UFO conspiracies
jokes.explanation: Pure Kim - completely serious about asking the VP about aliens

jokes.text: Hey, you know what we could do? All the football players are going to these batting cages, like this goofy golf type place, and then afterwards, they're gonna go swim in this lake.
jokes.setup: Sam asking Cindy on a real date
jokes.punchline: Cindy's idea of a good time involves following football players around
jokes.explanation: Shows Cindy's social priorities and why she and Sam are incompatible

jokes.text: I get it. You want to take your girlfriend out like a real gentleman.
jokes.setup: Sam wanting to plan their own date
jokes.punchline: Cindy's patronizing response treating Sam like a child playing at being a gentleman
jokes.explanation: Shows the condescending dynamic in their relationship

jokes.text: You can't even look at me! / I'm looking at you.
jokes.setup: Amy accusing Ken of not being able to look at her
jokes.punchline: Ken's literal, unhelpful response
jokes.explanation: Classic Ken literal-mindedness when emotional intelligence is needed

jokes.text: Live with what? It's over. I mean, you know, move on.
jokes.setup: Amy asking if Ken can live with her intersex history
jokes.punchline: Ken's dismissive 'move on' response to something deeply personal
jokes.explanation: Painfully accurate male emotional avoidance, delivered with Ken's characteristic bluntness

jokes.text: [Bad English accent] If it were me, I'd take her to a Broadway show. / Broadway? We're in Michigan. / [Normal voice] Dinner theater?
jokes.setup: Neal giving dating advice with a fake accent
jokes.punchline: His quick pivot to dinner theater when called out on geography
jokes.explanation: Nice physical comedy with the accent work and quick recovery

jokes.text: Laughter is the ultimate aphrodisiac. Get a woman laughing and you've got a woman loving.
jokes.setup: Neal explaining why comedy is good for dates
jokes.punchline: His pseudo-wisdom about laughter being an aphrodisiac
jokes.explanation: Classic teenage boy pseudo-wisdom delivered with complete confidence

jokes.text: Judith has very particular tastes. About the only thing she ever wants is scented oils and plenty of time with her man. / ECCH!
jokes.setup: Asked about gifts for his girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Harris oversharing about his girlfriend's intimate preferences
jokes.explanation: Perfect Harris TMI moment that grosses out everyone appropriately

jokes.text: After all, this is your first love.
jokes.setup: Sam's mom giving him the family necklace for Cindy
jokes.punchline: Her assumption that this teenage relationship is 'first love'
jokes.explanation: Parental romantic delusion that will soon be proved wrong

jokes.text: Amy's not really a girl. But she's kinda-- I mean, you know, she is-- kinda part guy, too.
jokes.setup: Ken trying to explain Amy's intersex condition to his friends
jokes.punchline: His completely inappropriate and confused explanation
jokes.explanation: Painfully realistic portrayal of teenage boy trying to process complex information

jokes.text: It means that she was packing both a gun and the holster.
jokes.setup: Ken trying to explain intersex anatomy
jokes.punchline: His crude metaphor using gun terminology
jokes.explanation: Crude but economical metaphor that shows Ken's discomfort and immaturity

jokes.text: You better get rid of her. / I don't want to break up with her. I really like her. / I might even...love her.
jokes.setup: Nick advising Ken to dump Amy
jokes.punchline: Ken's progression from liking to potentially loving her despite the revelation
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Ken's genuine feelings overcoming confusion

jokes.text: Well, there's a small little chance that... I might be gay.
jokes.setup: Ken coming to Rosso for advice
jokes.punchline: His tentative confession about questioning his sexuality
jokes.explanation: Brave storytelling moment handled with sensitivity and realism

jokes.text: I thought you'd be a good person to talk to about it, since you're gay. / I'm not gay.
jokes.setup: Ken assuming Rosso is gay
jokes.punchline: Rosso's flat denial
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection that creates maximum awkwardness for both characters

jokes.text: You just-- you kinda have this way about you, and I've never seen you with a woman.
jokes.setup: Rosso asking why Ken thought he was gay
jokes.punchline: Ken's honest but inappropriate reasoning
jokes.explanation: Realistic teenage logic about adult behavior, awkwardly delivered

jokes.text: I don't bring dates to school.
jokes.setup: Ken's reasoning about never seeing Rosso with women
jokes.punchline: Rosso's obvious response about professional boundaries
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response to Ken's faulty logic

jokes.text: Rosso singing 'If They Could See Me Now' while trying to break into his car
jokes.setup: Rosso locked out of his car
jokes.punchline: Singing an upbeat show tune about success while failing at basic tasks
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy contrasting upbeat song with mundane failure

jokes.text: What is your favorite place to eat in the state of Michigan?
jokes.setup: Lindsay expecting to ask hard-hitting political questions
jokes.punchline: The incredibly banal question Bush's people wrote for her
jokes.explanation: Perfect satire of political theater - reducing serious discourse to restaurant preferences

jokes.text: That's code for 'nice try, but this is a glorified photo opportunity.'
jokes.setup: Bush people calling Lindsay's questions too 'sophisticated'
jokes.punchline: Rosso's cynical translation of political spin
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire delivered with perfect world-weary Rosso cynicism

jokes.text: Maybe if I'm lucky, he'll tell me which steakhouse has the best prime rib.
jokes.setup: Lindsay's disappointment with the restaurant question
jokes.punchline: Her sarcastic response about the depth of discourse she can expect
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic response showing Lindsay's intelligence and frustration

jokes.text: 16 scars on my head from a tear-gas canister.
jokes.setup: Rosso reflecting on his protest days at Berkeley
jokes.punchline: The specific, violent result of his idealistic activism
jokes.explanation: Dark and specific detail that shows the real cost of his youthful idealism

jokes.text: And now all my compatriots are gettin' rich working Wall Street, and I can't get my keys out of my mother's car.
jokes.setup: Rosso's continued reflection on how things turned out
jokes.punchline: The contrast between his friends' success and his current pathetic situation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant encapsulation of middle-aged disappointment - perfect structure and character insight

jokes.text: Republicans aren't selfish. It's just that they don't believe poor people should get handouts. I mean, they should get jobs. Handouts just make them lazy, don't you think?
jokes.setup: Cindy explaining Republican philosophy to Sam
jokes.punchline: Her naive parroting of talking points while claiming Republicans aren't selfish
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Cindy's political naivety and privilege

jokes.text: And speaking of handouts, ha ha, uh, I got you something.
jokes.setup: Conversation about political handouts
jokes.punchline: Sam's terrible transition to giving Cindy a gift
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awkward Sam moment - trying to be smooth but failing completely

jokes.text: How much did it cost? / Um, I don't know. It's an heirloom.
jokes.setup: Sam gives Cindy the family necklace
jokes.punchline: Cindy immediately asking about cost, missing the sentimental point entirely
jokes.explanation: Perfect character clash - Sam's sentimentality vs. Cindy's materialism

jokes.text: You know, it's metal, so it'd be cold on my neck and everything.
jokes.setup: Sam offering to put the necklace on Cindy
jokes.punchline: Cindy's lame excuse for not wearing it
jokes.explanation: Painfully realistic excuse that shows Cindy doesn't want to wear the ugly necklace

jokes.text: Mom, busy doing what? Waiting for the president to die so he can take over?
jokes.setup: Jean saying Bush is a busy man
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's cynical view of the Vice President's actual duties
jokes.explanation: Sharp political humor delivered with perfect teenage cynicism

jokes.text: Your only affiliation right now to any party is to the Weir party.
jokes.setup: Harold pressuring Lindsay to mention his store
jokes.punchline: His wordplay on political parties vs. the family name
jokes.explanation: Classic dad joke that's actually quite clever in context

jokes.text: He hates these cans! Stay away from the cans!
jokes.setup: Sam and Cindy watching The Jerk
jokes.punchline: The famous absurd line from Steve Martin's movie
jokes.explanation: Classic comedy bit that serves the plot - Sam laughs, Cindy doesn't

jokes.text: Do you want some popcorn or something? / Will popcorn make this funny?
jokes.setup: Sam trying to salvage the date experience
jokes.punchline: Cindy's blunt assessment that nothing can save this movie for her
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery that shows their fundamental incompatibility

jokes.text: How old is this guy? He's got gray hair, and he's running around like a 5-year-old.
jokes.setup: Cindy watching Steve Martin in The Jerk
jokes.punchline: Her literal-minded criticism of physical comedy
jokes.explanation: Shows Cindy's inability to appreciate absurdist humor - she wants everything literal

jokes.text: I'm giving you a hickey, you goof.
jokes.setup: Sam confused about what Cindy is doing during the movie
jokes.punchline: Cindy's explanation of her physical advances
jokes.explanation: Shows the physical awkwardness of teenage relationships

jokes.text: Yeah, if you're attracted to guys that look like Jesus. / Shut up!
jokes.setup: Kim saying Rosso is good-looking
jokes.punchline: Daniel's comparison to Jesus
jokes.explanation: Good visual joke that's both accurate and dismissive in typical Daniel fashion

jokes.text: YEAH, IF YOU'RE ATTRACTED TO GUYS THAT LOOK LIKE JESUS.
jokes.setup: Kim saying Rosso is kind of good-looking
jokes.punchline: Daniel's sarcastic comparison to Jesus
jokes.explanation: Good Daniel line - the Jesus comparison is both accurate and dismissive. His jealousy about Kim's attraction comes through.

jokes.text: Ken punches Daniel in the face
jokes.setup: Tension building from Ken's emotional state
jokes.punchline: Sudden violence between friends
jokes.explanation: Shocking moment that shows Ken's emotional turmoil through physical action

jokes.text: How come I don't like her? / Sam, just 'cause a girl's pretty doesn't mean you have to like her.
jokes.setup: Sam's confusion about not liking the beautiful Cindy
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's wise response about superficial attraction
jokes.explanation: Good sibling wisdom moment with universal truth about relationships

jokes.text: Well, because they can't believe that... she's going out with me in the first place.
jokes.setup: Lindsay asking why Sam won't break up with Cindy
jokes.punchline: Sam's honest admission about his self-esteem issues
jokes.explanation: Painfully honest teenage self-awareness delivered with perfect vulnerability

jokes.text: You better wear a turtleneck before Mom and Dad see that.
jokes.setup: Lindsay noticing Sam's hickey
jokes.punchline: Her practical advice about hiding it from parents
jokes.explanation: Good sibling moment that sets up future visual comedy

jokes.text: I'm glad your stomach hurts. / You know what that is? It's your body telling you you're making a big mistake.
jokes.setup: Sam feeling sick about breaking up with Cindy
jokes.punchline: Neal's pseudo-scientific explanation of anxiety
jokes.explanation: Typical Neal overconfidence in dispensing relationship wisdom

jokes.text: Years from now you're gonna be sitting in your house, looking at your unattractive kids with your unattractive wife, saying to yourself, 'Man, why did I ever dump that goddess Cindy Sanders?'
jokes.setup: Neal trying to convince Sam not to break up with Cindy
jokes.punchline: His vision of Sam's bleak future with ugly family
jokes.explanation: Classic Neal dramatic pessimism with specific visual details

jokes.text: Have I ever told you about the time that I made out with Vicki Appleby? / Oh, shut up. You're such a liar.
jokes.setup: Bill claiming romantic experience
jokes.punchline: Neal's immediate dismissal of the obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bill's fantasy life and Neal's skepticism

jokes.text: Did you just puke? / No. I'm nervous.
jokes.setup: Sam coming out of bathroom stall looking sick
jokes.punchline: His distinction between vomiting and being nervous
jokes.explanation: Good nervous energy moment showing Sam's physical anxiety

jokes.text: I'm gonna break up with Cindy Sanders. / Wow. Why, man? She's hot.
jokes.setup: Sam announcing his breakup plans
jokes.punchline: Ken's immediate focus on the superficial
jokes.explanation: Typical male response prioritizing physical attraction over compatibility

jokes.text: You serious? / Man, my girlfriend loved The Jerk.
jokes.setup: Sam complaining Cindy thought The Jerk was stupid
jokes.punchline: Ken's response highlighting how his girlfriend appreciated the movie
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that contrasts the two relationships' compatibility

jokes.text: Sam, you're wearing a turtleneck. / Are you covering up my hickey?
jokes.setup: Sam wearing turtleneck as Lindsay suggested
jokes.punchline: Cindy immediately figuring out why
jokes.explanation: Good visual callback that sets up the relationship tension

jokes.text: So I guess if you got me a diamond ring and I didn't wear it because I was too embarrassed, you wouldn't get all mad, either?
jokes.setup: Cindy angry about Sam hiding the hickey
jokes.punchline: Her sarcastic comparison to hiding expensive jewelry
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Cindy's materialistic values and hurt feelings

jokes.text: Hey, come on! I got you a necklace, and you've never worn it. / It was ugly!
jokes.setup: Sam defending himself about the hickey situation
jokes.punchline: Cindy's brutally honest assessment of the heirloom necklace
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation with Cindy's blunt honesty destroying Sam's romantic gesture

jokes.text: You're supposed to be nice. That's the only reason why I'm going out with you in the first place.
jokes.setup: Sam breaking up with Cindy
jokes.punchline: Cindy's honest admission about why she dated him
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest line that reveals the shallow foundation of their relationship

jokes.text: Have your stupid heirloom back.
jokes.setup: Breakup scene reaching its climax
jokes.punchline: Cindy's final dismissal of Sam's sentimental gift
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic escalation that definitively ends their relationship

jokes.text: Sir, are you now or have you ever been a member of an organization called the Taft Students Alliance for a New America?
jokes.setup: Secret Service screening Rosso for the Bush event
jokes.punchline: The McCarthy-era style questioning about student organizations
jokes.explanation: Good political satire referencing McCarthyism in 1980s context

jokes.text: I guess I rocked the boat a little bit in my youth.
jokes.setup: Rosso being detained as a security risk
jokes.punchline: His mild characterization of his radical activist past
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement given his earlier stories about tear gas injuries

jokes.text: You ever heard of the vice president getting assassinated? No. You know why? It's never happened. Will it ever happen? No way. Because who cares?
jokes.setup: Agent talking to Rosso about his job
jokes.punchline: His cynical assessment of the VP's importance and assassination risk
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny and surprisingly honest assessment from someone who should be taking it seriously

jokes.text: Sometimes I think I just want to rip off my vest and jacket and just put on a T-shirt and go and, like, make pancakes somewhere or something.
jokes.setup: Agent continuing his existential crisis
jokes.punchline: His specific fantasy of escaping to make pancakes
jokes.explanation: Beautifully specific absurd fantasy that humanizes the stone-faced agent

jokes.text: My question is... Why did your staff reject my question? Are you afraid of an open discourse with the students?
jokes.setup: Lindsay supposed to ask about restaurants
jokes.punchline: Her going completely off-script to challenge Bush directly
jokes.explanation: Perfect climactic moment - Lindsay rebels against political theater with genuine courage

jokes.text: "DO YOU LIKE WORKING WITH MAJOR APPLIANCES?" THAT'D BE A YES.
jokes.setup: Agent giving Rosso career aptitude test
jokes.punchline: Bizarre question and Rosso's resigned acceptance
jokes.explanation: Absurd ending to the agent/Rosso relationship. The random career test question caps their unlikely bonding moment.

jokes.text: Do you like working with major appliances? / That'd be a yes.
jokes.setup: Rosso taking a career aptitude test
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact response to an absurd career question
jokes.explanation: Nice absurdist ending showing Rosso's resigned acceptance of his fate
